content:
{
  "title": "Self-Consistency Guided Prompting for Enhanced Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often produce mathematically incorrect solutions due to reasoning inconsistencies and brittle prompt engineering. Existing prompting techniques fail to systematically encourage verification or refinement of intermediate results.",
  "motivation": "Improving mathematical problem solving is crucial for LLM deployment in STEM domains, but current models struggle with multi-step reasoning and error detection. Recent advances in self-consistency-based decoding suggest that leveraging internal model feedback can improve reliability. Prompting methods that explicitly integrate self-consistency checks remain underexplored, especially for math tasks.",
  "method": "We propose a prompting framework where, after an initial solution is generated, the LLM is prompted to independently solve the problem multiple times with slight paraphrasings. Subsequently, the model is given all generated answers and asked to identify the most consistent or majority result, optionally explaining divergent reasoning steps. This prompt sequence explicitly guides the model toward self-verification and majority voting within a single inference pipeline, without model fine-tuning.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate on GSM8K, MATH, and MultiArith datasets, comparing our self-consistency guided prompting to standard few-shot and chain-of-thought prompting. Metrics will include final answer accuracy, consistency rate across generations, and error analysis of reasoning steps. We will further analyze performance on problems of varying complexity and ablate the impact of the number of independent generations."
}
